title: dryopteris cambrensis subsp. insubrica
intro: Dryopteris cambrensis subsp. insubrica, also known as the Italian scaly male fern, is a subspecies of fern native to the Italian Peninsula and the surrounding regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Dryopteris cambrensis subsp. insubrica is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
